title: Even as They Praise Iran Cease-Fire, World Leaders Are Whipsawed by Trump
summary: Across Europe and the globe, the war has damaged economies, roiled politics and underscored a lack of options in dealing with President Trump’s whims.

title: Oil Prices Plunge and Stocks Surge After Cease-Fire Deal
summary: Markets reacted positively after the United States and Iran reached a last-minute cease-fire agreement.

title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
summary: The agreement to pause the fighting for two weeks came hours after President Trump threatened to destroy Iran’s “whole civilization.” Much remains uncertain.

title: Trump Backs Down on Iran, and an A.I. ‘Reckoning’
summary: Plus, the great pistachio gamble.

title: Georgia, Wisconsin Elections Show Declining Appetite for Republican Candidates
summary: A Republican won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s seat, but Democrats shifted the district 25 points to the left since the 2024 presidential race. Conservative candidates lost in Wisconsin, too.

title: Stung by Voters, Republican Legislators Move to Curb Citizen Initiatives
summary: After citizens in Republican states used ballot measures to protect abortion, expand Medicaid and raise the minimum wage, statehouses are moving to make such initiatives much harder.

title: In Georgia’s 14th District Special Runoff, Democrats Pulled Off Their Biggest Shift Yet
summary: Shawn Harris lost by about 12 percentage points in the 14th Congressional District, but he shifted the district 25 points to the left.

title: The Best Restaurants in Boston
summary: It’s not just seafood and Italian in the greater Boston area. There’s great Vietnamese, omakase, Peruvian and even bagels worth seeking out. (Don’t worry, there’s also great seafood and Italian.) We’ll see you in the comments.

title: The Best Restaurants in Philadelphia
summary: The dining scene here is having a moment, whether it’s outstanding pizza, fiery Ethiopian fried chicken or French inflected cuisine that feels like a cozy dinner party.

title: The 25 Best Restaurants in Chicago
summary: We scouted the city’s vast food scene, from renowned Italian beefs to tavern-style pizza to Michelin-starred tasting menus. (And we’re here for your comments.)

title: To Boost Military Budget, Trump Targets Popular Programs at Home
summary: Amid the war with Iran, the president has proposed to scale back some of the very programs meant to ease families’ financial burdens.

title: Only Trump Knows Why Bondi Was Fired as Attorney General, Blanche Says
summary: In his first news conference since being elevated to acting attorney general, Todd Blanche said that “nobody has any idea” what led to Pam Bondi’s dismissal other than President Trump.

title: Poll Shows Optimism in New York’s Future With Mamdani as Mayor
summary: A new Marist poll found that about half of New Yorkers approved of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s job performance, while a larger share of respondents believed the city was moving in the right direction.

title: How’s He Doing? 13 New Yorkers Weigh In on Mamdani’s First 100 Days.
summary: We talked to voters in neighborhoods where Zohran Mamdani is revered, in areas where he is feared and in diverse parts of the city where opinions differ.

title: ‘D.E.I.’ Was Erased From N.Y.C. Racial Equity Plan to Avoid Conflict With Trump
summary: The changes came as President Trump has moved to erase diversity, equity and inclusion efforts. A Justice Department official still said the report seemed “fishy/illegal.”

title: How China Built Its Vast Natural Gas Stockpile
summary: Natural gas is hard to store, but China has found a way to do it, while also developing alternate suppliers and expanding production at home.

title: What Do Seasonal Allergies Do to the Body?
summary: A breakdown of why your immune system treats pollen like a parasite — and what happens next.

title: He Got Rich Buying and Selling Luxury Watches. Was It a Ponzi Scheme?
summary: Dominic Khoo made waves in Singapore as an investor in expensive timepieces. Now many of his clients accuse him of fraud.

title: Local Landscaper Tackles the Hamptons Bird Flu Problem
summary: This winter, hundreds of dead geese fell around Georgica Pond. No one knew how to dispose of the infected bodies, so a local landscaper just winged it.

title: A.I. May Worsen Wealth Inequality
summary: A.I. will further enrich the winners and impoverish the losers, with inevitable societal impacts.

title: Did Wokeness Leave Us Worse Off?
summary: The debate over words we can and can’t say.

title: A Near Miss
summary: We look at the cease-fire and the discussions that led to war.

title: Late Night Puzzles Over Trump’s ‘Mixed Message’ on Iran
summary: “Everyone, most notably the people of Iran, were wondering if their civilization was going to die tonight. Well, good news, it didn’t,” Jimmy Kimmel said after a cease-fire was announced.

title: Defendant in Gilgo Beach Murders May Plead Guilty on Wednesday
summary: Rex Heuermann, who is charged with murdering seven women dating back to the 1990s, was arrested in 2023 and has pleaded not guilty. His trial is set to start after Labor Day.

title: Gracie Mansion Suspects Wanted to Kill Up to 60 People, U.S. Says
summary: An indictment charged two men with conspiracy to use a weapon of mass destruction in the homemade bomb attack near Mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence. It cited dashcam audio in which one said, “I want to start terror bro.”

title: Heat Wipes Out Western Snowpack, Raising Fears of Summer Drought
summary: A record-mild winter and blistering spring heat will mean higher risks of water shortages and wildfires in the months ahead.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Get a Break After Journey Around the Moon
summary: The crew of the NASA mission had a quiet day as they flew home toward Earth.
